mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration (measured by GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes) drives positive equity valuations through improved corporate earnings expectations. When GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, S&P 500 forward returns are positive over the subsequent 20 trading days due to strengthened demand fundamentals.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth drives corporate earnings expectations
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of instances when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation, as higher rates compress equity valuations relative to risk-free alternatives.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate increases by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signal labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity sell-offs. The mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the magnitude of negative returns following UNRATE spikes is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth and retail sales growth move together to signal broad-based economic momentum. When both INDPRO and RSAFS show month-over-month growth simultaneously, supply-side capacity utilization and demand-side consumption strength reinforce each other, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved profit margins and revenue growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 0% AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Synchronized supply-demand growth signals broad expansion
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of co-positive INDPRO/RSAFS instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% year-over-year, signaling weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand destruction. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as construction and materials sectors face reduced demand, with spillover effects to consumer discretionary spending.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when HOUST YoY decline exceeds 10%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate interact such that when inflation is elevated (> 3.5% YoY) AND FEDFUNDS is rising, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This joint condition produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors fear growth-killing rate hikes and margin compression from stagflationary pressures.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.5% AND FEDFUNDS rising > 0.25pp in 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) when controlling for individual CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS effects.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100,000 jobs per month on a 3-month moving average, signaling labor market weakening and reduced consumer income growth. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as lower wage income reduces discretionary spending and equity risk appetite.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when PAYEMS 3-month growth falls below 100k, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward returns is negative but not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both accelerate simultaneously (YoY growth > 3% for RSAFS, > 5% for HOUST), signaling robust consumer and residential demand. This dual-strength condition produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations upward and cyclical sectors outperform.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth > 3% AND HOUST YoY growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:Synchronized demand acceleration signals strong cycle
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with synchronized demand strength
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of co-positive RSAFS/HOUST instances, or interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (R² improvement < 0.01) over single-series models.
